## [03:02 - 06:05]

That sequence between cart open and cart close, that's your launch sequence. And this is, a lot of times there's some real blocking and tackling to be done. That's for those, for everyone outside the US, that's an analogy to American football. Blocking and tackling is like, if you look at American football, it looks really complicated. And I can't imagine, I grew up with it. If I can't imagine being like an adult trying to watch it for the first time, because it's complex. But at the basic core of it is what they call the blocking and tackling. Like knocking over the other guys or having them knock over your guy. So that term blocking and tackling is just a term for the, let's get down to the bare fundamentals. And a lot of times it was in the launch, it is just blocking and tackling and taking care of business and working from the time you open that cart till the time you close cart to keep up, sort of keep up the pressure and keep on building that pressure right until the time when you close cart. And it is important that you do close the cart. This is a sequence. You don't wanna just have that cart open up and it stay open forever. And I mean, the cart can stay open forever, but the offer, your launch offer has to have a defined end. So I wanna be real clear on this. At the end of your open cart period, something bad has to happen if people don't buy from you. So either the product goes away and they can't get it anymore or the price goes up or bonuses go away. There's some defined end to your open cart period. If you don't do that, you're only gonna get half the sales you should have. You will literally double your sales if you have some defined end to your launch. So it's critically important. Or I guess it's only important if you wanna get all your sales. If you only wanna get 50% of the sales out there, then don't worry about closing the cart or closing the offer, closing the launch offer. So then after your launch, okay, you figure you're done, right? Well, then there is what we call the post launch. And this is where you take care of all your new clients. And this is where you start to set up your next launch because by taking care of your clients and by taking care of any partners, any JV partners, any content partners during that sequence and making good on all your promises, that's where you set up the next launch. It's critically important. A lot of times people get down to here and they're just, they're like so excited to be done that they forget about following up. The post launch is critical for your next launch. Also, you wanna really encourage the conversation within your post launch period as you're delivering the product because that conversation will give you the ideas for your next product, your next promotion. You also wanna be following up with the new prospects you have the people that didn't buy from you. You've been sending them really cool stuff all along. You don't wanna just go away and leave them alone for months and be like, okay, I thought he loved me but now I never hear from him anymore. So you don't wanna do that. You wanna have a plan for your post launch to follow up with your new buyers, with your prospects
